course_description:
This integrative, capstone course is concerned with the interplay of accounting strategy and organisational policy and strategy making.  How accounting strategy supports the achievement of the strategic ambitions of organisations is a particular focus.  Key issues are establishing effective accounting strategies and the organisational and environmental precursors to developing such strategies.  The distinction between financial and managerial accounting is given less emphasis; flexibility in the analysis of organisational opportunities is given more emphasis.  Students are expected to show intellectual leadership by conducting a significant research project within the domain of accounting.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
restrictions: Capstone course to be studied in the major. Course is restricted to students enrolled in the BAdvBus(Hons) program.

course_aims: This course will introduce you to research in accounting. Accounting is the language of business, which helps build trust in the business world by reducing information asymmetry and facilitating governance. The aim of the accounting discipline is to advance our knowledge in the information and governance role played by accounting information. This involves using analytical, empirical archival, experimental, and field study methods and addresses accounting questions, external and internal, in accounting, auditing, disclosure, financial reporting, corporate governance, etc. More specifically, the course will develop your capabilities to undertake research in the accounting discipline. You will learn various research methods and study published research papers in which these research methods have been used. You will learn to evaluate how well the research methods have been used in published research papers. You will also develop an understanding of some of the exciting, leading-edge research in the accounting discipline. This understanding may enable you to identify research topics that you would like to pursue, perhaps in a master's or PhD thesis. The course will help you gain the following knowledge and skills:

learning_outcomes.description: Describe some major and enduring research topics in the accounting discipline.
Develop a research question that addresses current accounting issues based on theoretical and methodological considerations learned in the course.
Demonstrate abilities in analysing and critiquing accounting policies and practices from multiple perspectives.
Undertake research in the accounting discipline.
Demonstrate an understanding of the practical application of concepts and models of accounting.
Evaluate research papers in the accounting discipline.
Develop an understanding of how the application of the methods of business research can generate new insight into accounting.

assessment_details.title: Research Proposal
assessment_details.task_description: For the final assessment of this course, you will be required to submit a proposal outlining your research on a topic related to Accounting.Your proposal should focus on an emerging topic in the field that is expected to make an incremental contribution to existing literature.A proposal is a plan for a research project that you have yet to undertake, and its purpose is to assist you in clarifying and planning the steps you will take to complete it.AI Statement:Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task.
